name: Creole-Style Oven Pot Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the roast and insert garlic slivers on all sides.
2. Coat the roast with seasoned flour.
3. Place the meat in a large roasting pan with vegetable oil and onion; roast uncovered at 500°F for 30 minutes until browned on all sides.
4. Reduce temperature to 300°F.
5. Surround the meat with bell pepper, celery, carrots, tomatoes, parsley, bay leaves, and water; season with salt and pepper.
6. Cover the pan and return it to the oven; roast for 1.5 hours.
7. Add potatoes and roast an additional hour.
8. Remove the lid to brown vegetables and reduce stock; roast for about 30 minutes longer.
9. Adjust seasonings, transferring the roast and vegetables to a heated platter.
10. Heat the stock over direct heat until it browns and thickens the gravy.
11. Spoon the gravy over sliced meat and vegetables.
